entity.summary: UK-registered centralized digital-asset exchange whose corporate and regulatory position deteriorated sharply in 2026. OFAC designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd on 2026-01-30 for operating in Iran's financial sector and for materially supporting the IRGC. Companies House later dissolved the UK company through compulsory strike-off on 2026-05-12 after identifying misleading, false, or deceptive incorporation information. HEI classifies Zedxion as limited rather than dead because its public exchange website remained accessible and continued to advertise registration and trading after the corporate dissolution date.
entity.notes: The launch date uses the UK entity's incorporation date as the earliest authoritative public-lineage marker; an independently verified exact trading-launch date has not been established. status is limited because the legal entity was dissolved and the exchange was designated by OFAC, but the public website still presented active exchange services as of verification. death_reason and death_date remain null until operational cessation is directly established.

events.title: Zedxion's UK corporate exchange lineage was established
events.description: Zedxion Exchange Ltd was incorporated in the United Kingdom on 2021-05-17. OFAC later identified it as a UK-registered digital-asset exchange and used the same establishment date in its designation record.
events.notes: This is a corporate-registration marker used as the earliest authoritative lineage date, not a claim that trading necessarily opened on the same day.

events.title: OFAC designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd
events.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd under Iran-sector and counterterrorism authorities. Treasury stated that Zedxion-linked addresses had processed funds for wallets linked to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and designated the exchange for operating in Iran's financial sector and materially supporting the IRGC.
events.notes: The designation subjected the named UK company and listed exchange identity to U.S. sanctions and secondary-sanctions risk.

events.title: Companies House dissolved Zedxion Exchange Ltd
events.description: Companies House recorded the final compulsory strike-off and dissolution of Zedxion Exchange Ltd on 2026-05-12. The registrar stated that the action related to incorporation information or statements considered misleading, false, or deceptive. The public exchange website nevertheless remained accessible after the dissolution date, so HEI records a limited rather than terminal dead-side status.
events.notes: Corporate dissolution is established, but operational shutdown is not. The distinction is preserved explicitly.

evidence.title: ZEDXION EXCHANGE LTD company overview
evidence.notes: Primary company-register source for company number, incorporation date, previous name, UK registration, and eventual dissolution.

evidence.title: Iran-related and counterterrorism designations dated January 30, 2026
evidence.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury — OFAC
evidence.notes: Primary sanctions-list record identifying Zedxion Exchange Ltd as a UK digital-asset exchange established on 2021-05-17.

evidence.title: Treasury sanctions Iranian regime officials and connected digital-asset exchanges
evidence.notes: Primary narrative source for the designation authorities, Zedxion's connection to Babak Morteza Zanjani, and Treasury's stated IRGC-linked transaction basis.

evidence.title: OFAC SDN entry for ZEDXION EXCHANGE LTD
evidence.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury — OFAC
evidence.notes: Primary designation entry listing the exchange website, company number, establishment date, sanctions programs, and IRGC linkage.

evidence.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard used crypto to evade sanctions, report finds
evidence.notes: Independent pre-designation reporting on blockchain analysis linking Zedxion infrastructure and transactions to IRGC-controlled wallets.

evidence.title: ZEDXION EXCHANGE LTD company overview after dissolution
evidence.notes: Primary source confirming dissolved status, the 2026-05-12 dissolution date, and the section 1002A basis concerning misleading, false, or deceptive incorporation information.

evidence.title: ZEDXION EXCHANGE LTD filing history
evidence.notes: Primary filing chronology showing the first Gazette notice, temporary suspension, and final Gazette compulsory dissolution on 2026-05-12.

evidence.title: Zedxion public exchange website after corporate dissolution
evidence.notes: Official public-facing site remained accessible and advertised account registration, spot and derivatives trading, exchange earn, and other services after the UK company dissolution date; this supports limited rather than dead status.
